provenance.authoritative_reason: local HEAD diverges from origin/main; working tree is dirty; perf_scoreboard.py modified vs HEAD

direction: speedup = cpython_time / molt_time; >1.0 = molt faster; <=1.0 = engine/cold red

verdict_legend.GREEN: warm fast, cold fast, within cold-start budget
verdict_legend.WARN_COLD_FLOOR: cold <= 1.00 but warm > 1.00 & tax within budget — not a hard red
verdict_legend.FAIL_STALE: non-authoritative tree — overrides all (gate fails unless --allow-nonauthoritative)
verdict_legend.UNSTABLE: CV above threshold — untrustworthy in either direction
verdict_legend.RUN_ERROR: CPython ran but molt did not
verdict_legend.CPY_INCOMPATIBLE: no CPython floor — excluded from gate

classify_legend.RED_STABLE: warm<1.00, repeat CI clears below 1.00, quiescent+stable — TRUE compiler target
classify_legend.RED_NOISY: warm<1.00 BUT contaminated/unstable/CI-straddles — NOT yet a target
classify_legend.TIE: repeat CI crosses 1.00 (or warm==1.00) — neither win nor loss
classify_legend.GREEN_STABLE: stable warm>1.00, quiescent — a won class
classify_legend.DIMENSIONAL_WIN: warm gate flat but alloc/RSS/size/cold improved >= 5% vs baseline (Rule 4)
classify_legend.INFRA: build/run/blocked/cpy-incompat/stale — no warm number to classify

cycle_profile.note: /usr/bin/sample 6s self-time over 3 back-to-back runs (CYCLES, not alloc-count)
cycle_profile.interpretation: LAUNCH/PAGE-IN-BOUND at this scale: the leaf self-time is dominated by _dyld_start (process launch + first-touch page-in of molts statically-linked binary), with ~0% in the molt user-binary text. molts AOT steady-state compute is faster than the per-process page-in cost, so single-process CPU sampling of these short benchmarks measures launch/page-in, not the steady-state hot loop. This CONFIRMS compute is not the bottleneck at this scale (consistent with COLD_START.md) but does NOT isolate the dataclass-field/dict-value-slot hot path for #68 -- that needs a workload that loops the hot region inside one warmed process, or a symbolicated build (release-fast strips molt user-fn symbols -> in-binary frames show as ???). #68 dataclass/dict hypothesis: NEITHER confirmed nor refuted by release-fast cycle sampling.
